title: Resyntha
shortDescription: Multi-Agent Research Intelligence Platform for automated literature review, multi-provider retrieval, structured knowledge extraction, research landscape generation, and research gap detection.

overview.problem: Researchers face information overload — manually reviewing hundreds of papers, extracting key findings, and identifying research gaps is slow and inconsistent.
overview.solution: Resyntha orchestrates multiple AI agents across a stage-based asynchronous pipeline to automate literature reviews: it searches academic sources via multi-provider retrieval, extracts structured knowledge, generates research landscapes, detects gaps, and provides an AI copilot for interactive exploration.

details.architecture: Production-oriented FastAPI backend with a stage-based pipeline engine driving asynchronous execution via ARQ and Redis. SQLAlchemy manages PostgreSQL persistence for versioned artifacts. React + TypeScript frontend with TanStack Query orchestrates real-time pipeline state. Multi-provider retrieval supports Semantic Scholar, OpenAlex, and arXiv. Groq accelerates LLM inference for extraction and analysis stages.
technicalChallenges: Designing a stage-based pipeline engine with deterministic validation between stages
Managing asynchronous task execution with Redis queue and result backpressure
Building versioned artifact storage with rollback and diff capabilities
Orchestrating multi-agent coordination without deadlock or duplicated work
Implementing caching strategies across API sources to respect rate limits
Supporting multi-provider retrieval with normalized result formats
details.learnings: Deepened understanding of pipeline architecture design, asynchronous task orchestration, multi-agent coordination patterns, building observability into distributed data workflows, and production-oriented backend architecture.

futureImprovements: Interactive research landscape visualization with graph exploration
Custom agent creation interface for domain-specific extraction
Collaborative review sessions with shared workspaces
Automated literature alerting and monitoring for new publications
Expanded source adapters for PubMed, IEEE, and Scopus
